title: 4.0 Energy Changes in Electrochemistry
description: Quiz covering Unit 4 of Grade 10 Chemistry: exothermic and endothermic reactions, energy changes, electrochemistry, electrical conductivity, electrochemical cells, and electrolysis.

questions.question: In an exothermic reaction, the enthalpy change ΔH is:
The device used to measure the heat released or absorbed by a chemical reaction is called a:
Endothermic reactions are characterized by:
Which of the following is an endothermic process according to the textbook?
Specific heat capacity is defined as the quantity of heat required to change the temperature of:
options: 1 mole of the substance by 1 K
1 kilogram of the substance by 1 K
1 gram of the substance by 1 K
questions.correctAnswer: 1 gram of the substance by 1 K

questions.question: Water has a high specific heat capacity, which makes it useful as:
options: a coolant in car radiators and industries
questions.correctAnswer: a coolant in car radiators and industries

questions.question: If 50 g of a substance with specific heat capacity 0.90 J/g·K is heated from 25°C to 45°C, the heat absorbed is:
The two principal advantages of chemical changes are:
options: production of energy and useful substances
production of new elements and compounds
questions.correctAnswer: production of energy and useful substances

questions.question: The relationship between specific heat capacity and heat capacity is given by:
options: specific heat capacity = heat capacity / mass
specific heat capacity = heat capacity × mass
specific heat capacity = mass / heat capacity
specific heat capacity = heat capacity × temperature
questions.correctAnswer: specific heat capacity = heat capacity / mass

questions.question: According to the textbook, which of the following reactions releases heat?
Electrochemistry is a field of chemistry that deals with:
options: the study of electrons in atoms
the production of electricity from heat
the interconversion of electrical energy and chemical energy
the extraction of metals from ores
questions.correctAnswer: the interconversion of electrical energy and chemical energy

questions.question: Electrical conductivity in metals is due to the movement of:
According to the textbook, the best electrical conductor among metals is:
Which non-metal is a good conductor of electricity due to freely moving electrons in its hexagonal layers?
In electrolytic conductivity, the charge carriers are:
Strong electrolytes dissociate completely in water, while weak electrolytes dissociate:
Which of the following is classified as a strong electrolyte in the textbook?
A nonelectrolyte is a substance that:
options: conducts electricity only in the molten state
conducts electricity only in aqueous solution
conducts electricity in both aqueous and molten states
does not conduct electricity in aqueous solution or in the molten state
questions.correctAnswer: does not conduct electricity in aqueous solution or in the molten state

questions.question: The conductivity of electrolytic solutions depends on:
options: the type and concentration of ions in solution
the volume of the solution only
the temperature of the solid solute only
questions.correctAnswer: the type and concentration of ions in solution

questions.question: A Galvanic cell or voltaic cell is an electrochemical cell in which:
options: heat energy is converted into electrical energy
electrical energy is converted into chemical energy
light energy is converted into chemical energy
chemical energy is converted into electrical energy
questions.correctAnswer: chemical energy is converted into electrical energy

questions.question: In a Daniell cell, the anode reaction is:
The purpose of the salt bridge in a Daniell cell is to:
options: increase the voltage of the cell
maintain electrical neutrality between the two solutions
questions.correctAnswer: maintain electrical neutrality between the two solutions

questions.question: Primary cells are galvanic cells that are:
options: made of lead and lead oxide

questions.question: An example of a primary cell according to the textbook is:
In a Leclanche dry cell, the cathode compartment contains manganese dioxide and:
The voltage of a single lead-storage cell is:
During the discharge of a Daniell cell, the zinc electrode:
During the discharge of a lead-storage battery, the overall cell reaction produces:
Electrolysis is a process by which:
options: electrical energy is used to drive a nonspontaneous reaction
chemical energy is converted into electrical energy
heat energy is converted into chemical energy
light energy is converted into electrical energy
questions.correctAnswer: electrical energy is used to drive a nonspontaneous reaction

questions.question: In an electrolytic cell, the electrode connected to the positive terminal of the source is called the:
During the electrolysis of molten NaCl, the half-reaction at the cathode is:
In an electrolytic cell, oxidation occurs at the:
During electrolysis, positive ions are attracted to the:
The electrolysis of molten NaCl is used commercially to obtain:
According to the textbook, which of the following metals is obtained by the electrolysis of a molten chloride?
